datenschutz.title: Privacy Policy
datenschutz.content:
1. Responsible Party

GreenHOM Energy GmbH
Brüsseler Straße 6
67657 Kaiserslautern
Germany

Represented by the managing directors:
Yannick Großmann, Dietrich Deschtschenja

Email: dsgvo@greenhom.energy
Website: www.greenhom.energy

2. General information on data processing

We process personal data of our users generally only insofar as this is necessary to provide a functional website as well as our content and services.
The processing of personal data regularly takes place only after the user's consent or if the processing is permitted by legal provisions.

3. Access data / Server log files

When calling up this website, our hosting provider Strato AG automatically collects data and information transmitted by your browser.
This includes:
- Browser type and browser version
- operating system used
- Referrer URL
- Hostname of the accessing computer
- Time of server request
- IP address

This data is stored for a maximum of 7 days and is evaluated exclusively to ensure trouble-free operation, system security, and to improve the offering.
Legal basis: Art. 6 Para. 1 lit. f GDPR (legitimate interest).

4. Use of cookies

Our website uses cookies. Some cookies are technically necessary, others serve statistical or marketing purposes.
You can deactivate the storage of cookies in your browser at any time or delete already stored cookies.

Legal basis:
- Art. 6 Para. 1 lit. a GDPR (consent) for optional cookies
- Art. 6 Para. 1 lit. f GDPR for technically necessary cookies

5. Newsletter

If you subscribe to our newsletter, we use your email address exclusively for sending the newsletter.
Registration takes place via a double opt-in procedure.
Legal basis: Art. 6 Para. 1 lit. a GDPR (consent).
You can revoke your consent at any time, for example via the unsubscribe link in the newsletter or by notifying dsgvo@greenhom.energy.

6. Integration of Google Maps

Our website uses the Google Maps map service of Google Ireland Limited (Gordon House, Barrow Street, Dublin 4, Ireland).
When used, data, in particular your IP address, is transferred to Google servers.
Legal basis: Art. 6 Para. 1 lit. a GDPR (consent).
Further information: https://policies.google.com/privacy

7. Social-Media-Links

Links to our social media profiles (e.g. LinkedIn, Instagram etc.) are integrated on our website.
When visiting our website, no automatic data transfer to these platforms takes place. A connection is only established when the respective link is clicked.
Legal basis: Art. 6 Para. 1 lit. f GDPR (legitimate interest in public relations).

8. Contact

If you contact us by email or contact form, your details will be stored for processing the inquiry and for follow-up questions.
We will not pass on this data without your consent.
Legal basis: Art. 6 Para. 1 lit. b GDPR (contract fulfillment or pre-contractual measures) or Art. 6 Para. 1 lit. f GDPR (legitimate interest).

9. Rights of the data subjects

You have the following rights with regard to the personal data concerning you:
- Right to information (Art. 15 GDPR)
- Right to rectification (Art. 16 GDPR)
- Right to erasure (Art. 17 GDPR)
- Right to restriction of processing (Art. 18 GDPR)
- Right to data portability (Art. 20 GDPR)
- Right to object (Art. 21 GDPR)
- Right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority (Art. 77 GDPR)

Competent supervisory authority:
State Commissioner for Data Protection and Freedom of Information Rhineland-Palatinate
Postfach 30 40, 55020 Mainz
https://www.datenschutz.rlp.de

10. Data security

We implement technical and organizational security measures to protect your data against manipulation, loss, destruction or access by unauthorized persons.

11. Topicality and amendment of this privacy policy

This privacy policy is currently valid and was last updated in October 2025.
Due to the further development of our website or changed legal requirements, it may become necessary to amend this declaration.
The current version can always be found on our website.

agb.title: General Terms and Conditions (GTC)
agb.content:
GreenHOMEnergy GmbH
Brüsseler Straße 6, 67657 Kaiserslautern
HRB 108855 – Local court Kaiserslautern
VAT ID: DE356021633
As of 01/2026

1. Scope of Application

1) These General Terms and Conditions (GTC) apply to all contracts, deliveries and services of GreenHOMEnergy GmbH (hereinafter "Contractor"), in particular as general contractor (GC), EPC, assembly partner or planning service provider in the field of photovoltaic ground-mounted systems, carport systems or rooftop systems.

2) Deviating, conflicting or supplementary conditions of the Client shall only become part of the contract if the Contractor has expressly agreed to their validity in writing.

3) Individual agreements take precedence over these GTC.

2. Scope of Services

1) The Contractor provides services according to agreement:
a) Work services (in particular as general contractor, EPC, assembly or planning service provider) or
b) pure goods deliveries of photovoltaic components (purchase contract).

2) If only components are delivered, the Contractor owes neither planning, design, system integration, assembly, commissioning nor the functionality of an overall system.

3) For pure goods deliveries, only the regulations of these GTC applicable to purchase contracts apply. Work contract regulations do not apply.

3. Conclusion of Contract & Credit Check

1) Offers from the Contractor are non-binding, unless expressly designated as binding. An order is only concluded after written order confirmation by the Contractor.

2) The Contractor is entitled to deliver equivalent components, provided that the agreed quality is not impaired thereby.

3) The Contractor is entitled to check the creditworthiness of the Client via credit agencies (e.g. Creditsafe) and, in the event of negative creditworthiness, to reject the conclusion of the contract or to demand a higher advance payment.

4. Prices & Payment Terms

1) Unless otherwise agreed, invoices are issued according to construction progress in the form of installment payments.

2) Payments are due within 14 calendar days without deduction. Discounts or other reductions are only granted if expressly agreed in writing.

3) Securities (e.g. guarantees) are only to be provided if expressly agreed in writing.

4) If the Client defaults on payment, the Contractor is entitled to demand default interest at the statutory rate.

5) If a due payment remains unpaid despite at least one written reminder and a renewed reminder, the Contractor is entitled, without further prior notice, to commission an external payment service provider or a collection agency to collect the claim. The costs incurred thereby shall be borne by the Client.

5. Delivery & Service Deadlines / Delays

1) Agreed deadlines and dates are only approximate, unless expressly guaranteed as binding.

2) Delays due to force majeure, weather conditions, permits, supply bottlenecks or on-site circumstances extend the deadlines accordingly.

3) Agreed completion dates will be met, provided that all necessary components are delivered on time and completely, and on-site conditions are met. Delays caused by late or incomplete deliveries of components or by circumstances beyond the control of the Contractor relieve the Contractor from compliance with promised deadlines.

4) Standstill costs due to on-site circumstances are charged at EUR 80.00 per employee per hour.

5) Additional costs for site setup due to bad weather will be invoiced separately.

6) If additional costs arise due to incorrect or incomplete planning documents of the Client or third parties, these shall be borne by the Client.

6. Acceptance & Handover

1) Services are deemed accepted if they are confirmed by the Client on site with an acceptance protocol and signature.

2) Partial acceptances are permissible and can be requested for self-contained services.

7. Liability & Warranty

1) For work services, the statutory warranty periods apply.

2) For pure goods deliveries to entrepreneurs (§ 14 BGB), the warranty period is 12 months from the transfer of risk, insofar as legally permissible.

3) The warranty extends exclusively to material defects of the delivered goods at the time of transfer of risk.

4) For work services, the Contractor is liable for planning and assembly errors only within the framework of legal regulations. The Client is obligated to inspect the delivered goods immediately upon delivery and to report recognizable defects in writing without delay. If the Client fails to give timely notice, the goods shall be deemed approved (§ 377 HGB).

5) For delivered components, guarantees from the respective manufacturers may exist. These guarantees exist exclusively between the manufacturer and the Client. The Contractor does not assume any guarantees of its own for delivered components and is not a guarantor. Statutory warranty claims of the Client against the Contractor remain unaffected.

6) For pure goods deliveries, in the case of subsequent performance, the Contractor owes exclusively the delivery of a defect-free item. Costs for removal, disassembly, reinstallation, assembly, scaffolding, lifting equipment or other ancillary services are not covered, insofar as legally permissible.

7) The Contractor is liable without limitation for intent and gross negligence; in case of simple negligence, liability is limited to the breach of material contractual obligations (cardinal obligations) and is in this case limited to the foreseeable, contract-typical damage.

8) Liability for lost profits, yield losses, standstill costs or other indirect damages is excluded, insofar as legally permissible. The above limitations of liability do not apply to injury to life, body or health, or to mandatory statutory liability.

8. Special Regulations for PV Systems

1) Ownership of delivered components remains with the Contractor until full payment of all claims, even if the goods have already been installed.

2) Weather damage to already delivered or installed components during the construction phase shall be borne by the Client, unless the Contractor caused the damage intentionally or through gross negligence.

3) The Client undertakes to comply with all safety regulations and the respective site regulations.

4) Should an agreed date for the delivery of goods not be met by the Client, the Contractor reserves the right to charge for the costs of a renewed delivery.

5) Information on average yields represents non-binding forecasts; no claims against the Contractor can be derived therefrom.

9. Final Provisions

1) Exclusively German law applies, excluding the UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods (CISG).

2) The place of jurisdiction, insofar as legally permissible, is the registered office of the Contractor in Kaiserslautern.

3) Should individual provisions of these GTC be or become invalid, the validity of the remaining provisions shall remain unaffected.

widerruf.title: Right of Revocation
widerruf.content:
Note:
This right of revocation only applies to contracts with consumers within the meaning of § 13 BGB. A consumer is any natural person who concludes a legal transaction for purposes that are predominantly neither commercial nor their independent professional activity.

There is no right of revocation for entrepreneurs within the meaning of § 14 BGB (legal persons, merchants, self-employed persons or other commercial traders).

Right of Revocation

You have the right to revoke this contract within 14 days without giving reasons.

The revocation period is 14 days from the day
- in the case of a sales contract: on which you or a third party named by you, who is not the carrier, took possession of the goods.
- in the case of a contract for several goods ordered as part of a single order and delivered separately: on which you or a third party named by you took possession of the last goods.
- in the case of a contract for the delivery of goods in several partial shipments or pieces: on which you or a third party named by you took possession of the last partial shipment or the last piece.
- in the case of a contract for the regular delivery of goods over a fixed period: on which you or a third party named by you took possession of the first goods.
- in the case of a service contract: from the conclusion of the contract.

To exercise your right of revocation, you must inform us

GreenHOMEnergy GmbH
Brüsseler Straße 6
67657 Kaiserslautern
info@greenhom.energy

by means of a clear declaration (e.g. a letter sent by post or an email) of your decision to revoke this contract.

You can use the attached sample revocation form for this, which is not mandatory.

To comply with the revocation period, it is sufficient that you send the notification of the exercise of the right of revocation before the expiry of the revocation period.

Consequences of Revocation

If you revoke this contract, we must repay you all payments that we have received from you, including delivery costs (with the exception of the additional costs resulting from the fact that you have chosen a type of delivery other than the cheapest standard delivery offered by us), without undue delay and at the latest within 14 days from the day on which the notification of your revocation of this contract has reached us.

For this repayment, we use the same means of payment that you used for the original transaction, unless something else was expressly agreed with you; in no case will you be charged fees for this repayment.

We may refuse repayment until we have received the goods back or until you have provided proof that you have returned the goods, whichever is the earlier.

You must return or hand over the goods to us without undue delay and in any case at the latest within 14 days from the day on which you inform us of the revocation of this contract. The deadline is met if you send the goods before the expiry of the 14-day period.

You bear the direct costs of returning the goods.

You only have to pay for any loss in value of the goods if this loss in value is due to handling of the goods that is not necessary for checking their condition, properties and functionality.

Exclusion or premature expiry of the right of revocation

The right of revocation does not apply to contracts
- for the delivery of goods that are not prefabricated and for the production of which an individual selection or determination by the consumer is decisive or which are clearly tailored to the personal needs of the consumer,
- for the delivery of goods that can spoil quickly or whose expiration date would be quickly exceeded,
- for the delivery of sealed goods that are not suitable for return for reasons of health protection or hygiene if their seal has been removed after delivery,
- for the delivery of goods if these have been inseparably mixed with other goods after delivery due to their nature,
- for the delivery of audio or video recordings or computer software in a sealed package, if the seal has been removed after delivery,
- for the delivery of newspapers, periodicals or magazines, with the exception of subscription contracts.

The right of revocation expires prematurely for contracts concerning
- the provision of services, if the entrepreneur has fully rendered the service and has only started to render the service after the consumer has given his express consent and at the same time confirmed his knowledge that he loses his right of revocation upon complete fulfillment of the contract by the entrepreneur,
- the delivery of digital content that is not delivered on a physical data carrier, if the entrepreneur has begun to execute the contract after the consumer has expressly consented and at the same time confirmed his knowledge that he loses his right of revocation with the beginning of the execution of the contract.

Sample Revocation Form

If you want to revoke the contract, please fill out this form and send it back.

To:
GreenHOMEnergy GmbH
Brüsseler Straße 6
67657 Kaiserslautern
info@greenhom.energy

I/we hereby revoke the contract concluded by me/us for the purchase of the following goods / the provision of the following service:
- Ordered on / received on: ………………………………………
- Name of consumer(s): ………………………………………
- Address of consumer(s): ………………………………………
- Signature of consumer(s) (only for notification on paper)
- Date: ………………………………………
